subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a new AWS Elastic Beanstalk application

options.description: Forces EB CLI to prompt you to provide a value for every eb init command option
The name of the AWS EC2 key pair
List of child directories to initialize
The platform version to use
List of tags to apply to the application

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploys the application source bundle from the initialized project directory to the running application

options.description: Specify a label to use for the version that the EB CLI creates

arg.name: version-label
arg.description: The label to use for the version

options.description: Group name to append to the environment name

arg.name: groupname
arg.description: The group name to append to the environment name

options.description: The description for the application version, enclosed in double quotation marks
List of components to update. Only for Compose Environments
Preprocess and validate the environment manifest and configuration files in the source bundle
CodeCommit repository and branch

arg.name: repository-name/repository-branch
arg.description: The name of the CodeCommit repository and branch

options.description: Deploy files staged in the git index instead of the HEAD commit
The number of minutes before the command times out
An existing application version to deploy

arg.name: version-label
arg.description: Label of the existing version to deploy

subcommands.name: clone
subcommands.description: Clones an environment to a new environment so that both have identical environment settings

options.description: Desired name for the cloned environment

arg.name: string
arg.description: The name for the cloned environment

options.description: Desired CNAME prefix for the cloned environment
Environment properties in a comma-separated list with the format name=value
Prevents Elastic Beanstalk from updating the solution stack version for the new clone environment to the most recent version available
The number of instances to run in the clone environment when it is launched
Tags for the resources in your environment in a comma-separated list with the format name=value

arg.name: name=value
arg.description: Tags for the resources in your environment

options.description: The number of minutes before the command times out

args.name: environment-name
args.description: The name of the environment to clone

subcommands.name: open
subcommands.description: Opens the public URL of your website in the default browser

args.name: environment-name
args.description: The name of the environment to open

options.description: List all environments from all applications
Provides more detailed information about all environments, including instances

subcommands.name: printenv
subcommands.description: Prints all the environment properties in the command window

args.name: environment-name
args.description: The name of the environment to print

subcommands.name: ssh
subcommands.description: Connect to a Linux Amazon EC2 instance in your environment using Secure Shell

options.description: The ID of the instance to connect to
The number of the instance to connect to by number
Leave port 22 open on the security group after the SSH session ends
Execute a shell command on the specified instance instead of starting an SSH session
Specify an SSH command to use instead of 'ssh -i keyfile'. Do not include the remote user and hostname
Change the key pair assigned to the environment's instances
Open port 22 to incoming traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 in the environment's security group, even if the security group is already configured for SSH
Set number of minutes before the command times out

args.name: environment-name
args.description: The name of the environment to connect to

subcommands.name: use
subcommands.description: Sets the specified environment as the default environment

options.description: Change the region in which you create environments
CodeCommit repository and branch

arg.name: codecommit/repository-name/repository-branch
arg.description: The name of the CodeCommit repository and branch

args.name: environment-name
args.description: The name of the environment to use
